objective_text: Evaluate a practical claim using source quality, date, evidence, and local applicability.
Use AI to summarize, compare, and critique sources without outsourcing judgment.
Produce a claim-check worksheet and a short cited recommendation note.
Choose and convert the units needed for common household calculations.
Map inputs, outputs, dependencies, and failure points in a household system.
Create a baseline resilience inventory covering needs, tools, contacts, and gaps.
Calculate daily and short-horizon household water needs in comparable units.
Compare treatment methods by source conditions, speed, cost, and limitations.
Build a practical storage table and treatment decision matrix for your context.
Run basic water testing or inspection steps and record the results clearly.
Convert storage, source, and treatment choices into a household water plan.
Explain when a plan should be retested, expanded, or revised.
Design a pantry layout around household needs, constraints, and likely gaps.
Set up a rotation and reorder cadence that prevents hidden waste.
Explain the safety boundaries of common preservation methods in plain language.
Start seeds with workable timing, medium, light, and moisture conditions.
Plan a small bed or container garden around space, season, and care capacity.
Translate garden goals into a layout, crop plan, and simple care calendar.
Explain the basic relationship between heat flow, air leakage, and the building envelope.
Inspect a space for likely weatherization priorities using a repeatable audit method.
Rank fixes by impact, effort, and constraints.
Identify common moisture sources and conditions that make mold more likely.
Use basic hand tools and household shutoffs with confidence and documentation.
Make simple seals, patches, and temporary repairs while recording what still needs a durable fix.
Differentiate volts, amps, watts, and watt-hours in plain language.
Read device labels and identify what information matters for planning.
Explain why breaker limits, startup surges, and real-world usage complicate simple guesses.
Separate essential, important, and optional loads for different outage durations.
Estimate runtime using consistent units, duty cycle, and efficiency assumptions.
Document the assumptions that most change your answer.
Draft low-, mid-, and high-budget backup options matched to critical loads and runtime goals.
Compare backup options by capability, burden, and local constraints.
Map local skills, tools, spaces, and sources that matter during normal times and disruptions.
Turn one completed capability into a short lesson with a clear promise, steps, and artifact.
Translate technical language into terms a general audience can follow.
Leave behind a handout, checklist, or map that remains useful without the instructor present.
